subtitle: Part of Marseille's largest beach — 3.5 km of free public sand the city built in the 1970s from the rubble excavated to make its metro — a democratic, multicultural anti-Riviera under a marble David, looking out to the Château d'If of Monte Cristo.
spike_statement: Marseille built its biggest beach — 3.5 km of free public sand — from the rubble dug out making its metro: a democratic, multicultural anti-Riviera under a marble David.

levers.photo_tone: a busy free public city beach on a bright Mediterranean blue, windsurfers out in the Mistral, the limestone Corniche and the city behind, the Château d'If small on the horizon, the white David statue at the roundabout
